summary: DHH shares a testimonial from Brazilian developer Ronaldo M. Ferraz who has fallen in love with Ruby on Rails despite his aesthetic objections to underscore characters. The post highlights Ferraz's praise for Rails' simplicity, intuitiveness, and ease of adding features like authentication. DHH uses this as an opportunity to celebrate Rails' growing international adoption, inviting readers to comment with their country to map the framework's global reach.
key_points: A Brazilian developer praises Rails for simplicity and intuitiveness, rarely needing documentation
Rails' ease of adding authentication to existing apps is highlighted as an 'epiphany' moment
DHH frames Rails adoption as a global movement, soliciting an informal roll call of countries
The post demonstrates early community-building tactics—amplifying user testimonials and fostering belonging
money_quotes: Rails is the best frameworks I've ever used. Things are always simple and intuitive, and I rarely need to resort to the documentation to understand how something is done.
I had an epiphany when I saw how easily I could add authentication to an application that was already working.
We're riding the Rails around the world to bring programming pleasure.
key_insight: DHH leveraged early international testimonials to build momentum and community identity around Rails as a global movement, not just a framework.
